checks.a_analysis: The output text has been compared against the source PDF. No fabricated paragraphs, sentences, or headers were identified. The metadata title and image alt text are expected AI-generated additions for accessibility and follow the guidelines for acceptable changes.

The output does not contain any fabricated contact information such as phone numbers, emails, or URLs. While the URL in the website section is omitted (addressed in C5), no incorrect or invented contact information was added to the document.

All primary sections of the agenda, including the council name, agenda title, date/time, the agenda table items 1 through 6, and the upcoming meeting dates, are represented in the output. No entire pages or primary sections are missing.

The source document contains one main agenda table consisting of a header row and six individual agenda item rows (numbered 1 through 6). The converted JSON represents this table with the same number of rows (1 header row and 6 data rows).

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The URL for the GICC website is missing from the final paragraph of the document. The source document explicitly lists 'https://it.nc.gov/gicc', whereas the output JSON only contains the label 'GICC Website: '. This omission constitutes a loss of more than 50% of the text for that specific element and removes critical contact/navigational information.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The structural representation of agenda item 5 ('Committee Reports') is flawed. In the source, the committee list and their presenters are contained within the table row for item 5. In the output, these details are extracted and placed after the entire table. This creates a reading order error where item 6 ('GICC Member Announcements') appears earlier in the flow than the sub-details of item 5. Furthermore, the explicit side-by-side association between committee names and their presenters is lost, as they are split into two separate, disjointed elements (an unordered list and a paragraph).
checks.b_evidence_source: The table structure shows Item 5 followed immediately by its sub-list of committees and presenters, preceding Item 6.
checks.b_evidence_output: The table includes Item 5 (header only) and Item 6, followed by a separate list of committees (reading_order 8) and a separate list of presenters (reading_order 9).
checks.b_evidence_location: Table element at reading_order 7, and subsequent elements 8 and 9

checks.a_analysis: The content of the document is not duplicated. Each section and agenda item appears only once in the JSON output, following the logical flow of the document (notwithstanding reading order issues discussed elsewhere).
The heading hierarchy is logical and follows a descending order: H1 for the top-level entity names, H2 for the 'AGENDA' title, and H3 for the 'Meeting Dates' section. There are no skipped levels.

The table structure in the JSON (element index 7) is corrupted. While items 1-4 and 6 are correctly placed in rows, the sub-items of item 5 ('Committee Reports') are excluded from the table and placed into separate List and Paragraph elements. In the source, these committees and the corresponding members are part of the three-column layout of the agenda. By separating them, the structural relationship between the committee and the responsible individual is broken.
checks.b_evidence_source:
Item 5. Committee Reports
• Statewide Mapping Advisory Paul Badr
• Local Government Debbie Brannan
...
checks.b_evidence_output: Element 7 (Table) Row 5 has content '5. Committee Reports' with an empty member cell. Element 8 (List) contains committee names. Element 9 (Paragraph) contains member names.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order is significantly disrupted due to the way item 5 was handled. The table (reading_order 7) contains Item 6 ('GICC Member Announcements') as its final row. However, the details for Item 5 ('Committee Reports') are listed in elements with reading_order 8 and 9. This forces a screen reader to read the entirety of Item 6 before hearing the details of Item 5, which is logically incorrect as Item 6 follows Item 5 in the source.
checks.b_evidence_source: Item 5 followed by item 6 on page 1.
checks.b_evidence_output: Item 6 is at reading_order 7 (within the table). Details of Item 5 are at reading_order 8 and 9.

checks.a_analysis: The committees under item 5 are correctly identified as an unordered list. Although they are disconnected from the names of the people, the list itself is structurally sound as a representation of the bullet points in the source.

The URL 'https://it.nc.gov/gicc' found at the bottom of the source document is missing from the JSON content. Element 12 contains the label 'GICC Website: ' but the actual URL text and link metadata are absent.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of Agenda Item 5 is significantly altered. In the source PDF, the committee names and their corresponding members are presented in a two-column alignment, clearly associating each committee with a person. In the HTML output, the table row for item 5 is mostly empty, and the detailed content of this section (the committees and names) has been moved below the entire table, including after Item 6. The committees are listed as a bulleted list, followed by a separate paragraph containing the names of the members. This rearrangement breaks the visual flow and makes it difficult to associate the information, which constitutes a major layout difference from the source's intent.
checks.b_evidence_source: Agenda Item 5 'Committee Reports' showing 6 committees in the left column aligned with 6 specific members in the right column.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML table shows Item 5 with an empty 'GICC MEMBER or GUEST' cell. Below the entire table (after item 6), committees are in a bulleted list, and then a paragraph of names: 'Paul Badr Debbie Brannan Scott Lokken Dean Grantham John Farley Alex Rankin'.
checks.b_evidence_location: Agenda table, item 5 and subsequent text blocks

checks.a_analysis: The table structure for Agenda Item 5 is visually corrupted. The content that should be within the table (the sub-items for committees and the names of the GICC members) has been ejected from the table structure. This results in missing data in the 'GICC MEMBER or GUEST' column for row 5 and the total loss of committee-to-member associations. The names are rendered as a plain text paragraph below the table instead of being correctly placed in columns or distinct rows, making the table data for this section unreadable as intended.
checks.b_evidence_source: A table where row 5 contains a list of committees and a corresponding list of members in distinct columns.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML table's 5th row is empty of member data. The data is rendered as text blocks outside of the table's structural context, with names appearing as a single run-on sentence.

checks.a_analysis: The meaningful NCGICC logo from the header of the source PDF is present in the HTML output. While it has been moved from the top of the document to the bottom, the image itself is correctly rendered and not broken.

There is no evidence of body content duplication. Section headings appear in both the Table of Contents and the document body, which is a standard accessibility feature and not a defect. No paragraphs or table segments are repeated within the body content.
